summary: chai typosquat fetches and executes remote JavaScript on require
details: Package impersonates chai (copies chai's description, homepage chaijs.com, repository chaijs/chai, and contributors in package.json; bundles a real chai 4.3.8 in lib/chai.js as cover). On require('chai-utils-test'), index.js (lines 7-13) spawns a detached, stdio-ignored Node child process running lib/invoker.js, then re-exports real chai so the consumer observes normal chai behavior. lib/invoker.js (lines 9-19) fetches https://statecheck.ddns.net/api/scanner.js over the network and executes the response body via `new Function('require', s)(require)`, handing full Node `require` capability to attacker-controlled code served from a dynamic-DNS host. The detached + stdio:ignore spawn is deliberate evasion (child outlives the parent and emits no output). Result: any consumer that imports this package executes whatever JavaScript the operator of statecheck.ddns.net chooses to serve at that moment, with full filesystem, network, and process access on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
